_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how authors select and vary text structures to create sequence and cohesion throughout a text.

_source.w: We are learning how authors select and vary text structures to create sequence and cohesion throughout a text.
y: Understanding structural selection and variation is key to creating sophisticated, well-crafted texts.
This knowledge helps us read and analyse how meaning is built across extended texts.
Mastering structural control is essential for the extended writing demands of senior secondary studies.
eg: We analyse how an author selects a particular text structure and then varies it strategically for effect.
We examine how structural choices create cohesion across a long text — essays, novels, reports.
We evaluate how varying structure within a text — like including a narrative within an argument — creates engagement.
We discuss how authors sequence their structural choices to build towards a particular conclusion or effect.
We create extended texts that demonstrate deliberate structural selection and variation.
We compare the structural strategies of texts in different genres and evaluate their effectiveness.
